scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every endpoint documented at api.akeneo.com/api-reference.html (products, products-uuid, product models, media files, families, family variants, attributes, attribute options, attribute groups, association types, categories, channels, locales, currencies, measure families, reference entities + sub-resources, asset families + sub-resources, catalogs, jobs, rule definitions, permissions, utilities, UI extensions, Data Architect Agent, workflows) is enumerated below. 12 GET list resources are streams (the original 5 plus product_models, family_variants, attribute_groups, association_types, locales, currencies, measure_families); 9 dialect-expressible upsert mutations are writes.json actions. Legacy was fully read-only (capabilities.write:false); this pass flips capabilities.write to true. Reference-entities and asset-manager sub-resources (each parameterized by a reference-entity/asset-family code this connector's spec.json has no config surface to enumerate), the UUID-keyed product variant of the same products resource, workflow/Data-Architect-Agent AI features, and catalogs export/import remain excluded (see per-endpoint reasons).

excluded.reason: OAuth2 password-grant token-exchange endpoint used internally by the akeneo AuthHook, not a syncable data stream or a directly caller-invoked write action
index endpoint listing the API's own available routes; not business data
returns Akeneo PIM edition/version metadata for introspection, not syncable business data
single-product detail; the products stream already covers the list data
identifier-only create; create_or_update_product's PATCH /products/{code} already creates (201) or updates (204) in one idempotent upsert call, matching Akeneo's own documented preferred pattern

bulk multi-product PATCH (newline-delimited JSON body, one product per line); create_or_update_product's single-product PATCH already covers create/update, one request per record, matching the engine's one-request-per-record write semantics (conventions.md §3)
permanently deletes a product and all its variant/history data with no undo; no obviously-safe reverse-ETL write surface for this pass
submits a draft for workflow approval; requires the Enterprise Edition workflow/proposal feature and reviewer-role context this connector's spec has no config surface for
Enterprise Edition draft/proposal detail, not available on every Akeneo edition
UUID-keyed variant of the same Product resource the products stream already lists via its identifier-keyed form; not separately migrated
UUID-keyed single-product detail; covered by the identifier-keyed products stream
UUID-keyed create; create_or_update_product's identifier-keyed PATCH already covers create/update
UUID-keyed bulk PATCH; not separately migrated from the identifier-keyed bulk exclusion above
server-side search over the same Product resource the products stream already lists in full
UUID-keyed single-product upsert; covered by create_or_update_product's identifier-keyed PATCH
permanently deletes a product; same reasoning as the identifier-keyed delete above
Enterprise Edition workflow/proposal feature
Enterprise Edition draft/proposal detail
single-product-model detail; the product_models stream already covers the list data
identifier-only create; create_or_update_product_model's PATCH already creates or updates in one idempotent upsert call
bulk multi-model PATCH; create_or_update_product_model's single-model PATCH already covers the same outcome, one request per record
permanently deletes a product model and orphans its variant products; no obviously-safe reverse-ETL write surface for this pass
Enterprise Edition workflow/proposal feature
Enterprise Edition draft/proposal detail

lists media-file metadata records referencing binary assets already attached to products/categories via the values/media-files write path; a thin metadata-only stream with no independent business meaning apart from the product/category it is attached to
multipart/form-data file upload; this dialect's write actions express only JSON/form bodies, not binary multipart uploads
single media-file metadata detail; excluded for the same reason as the list above
returns raw file bytes, not JSON record data
single-family detail; the families stream already covers the list data
identifier-only create; create_or_update_family's PATCH already creates or updates in one idempotent upsert call
bulk multi-family PATCH; create_or_update_family's single-family PATCH already covers the same outcome, one request per record
permanently deletes a family definition, affecting every product/product-model assigned to it; no obviously-safe reverse-ETL write surface for this pass
single-family-variant detail; the family_variants stream already covers the list data
identifier-only create; create_or_update_family_variant's PATCH already creates or updates in one idempotent upsert call
bulk multi-family-variant PATCH; create_or_update_family_variant's single-record PATCH already covers the same outcome, one request per record
single-attribute detail; the attributes stream already covers the list data
identifier-only create; create_or_update_attribute's PATCH already creates or updates in one idempotent upsert call
bulk multi-attribute PATCH; create_or_update_attribute's single-attribute PATCH already covers the same outcome, one request per record

measurement families have no per-code PATCH endpoint, only this bulk multi-family form (newline-delimited JSON body); the engine's one-request-per-record write semantics has no per-record analog to model here
